item.title: South Korea Tightens Property Curbs as Chip Boom Spurs Home Prices
item.description: South Korea imposed tougher property curbs in neighborhoods outside Seoul with proximity to semiconductor hubs after a boom in the industry fueled sharp home-price gains, raising concerns about speculative buying.

item.title: AirPods Maker Luxshare Seeks Up to $3.1 Billion From Hong Kong Listing
item.description: Apple Inc. supplier Luxshare Precision Industry Co. has started taking investor orders for its Hong Kong listing that may fetch as much as HK$24.3 billion ($3.1 billion), the city’s biggest share debut so far this year.

item.title: Tencent Ramps Up Buybacks as Share Rout Wipes Out $309 Billion
item.description: China’s Tencent Holdings Ltd. is ramping up buybacks as its Hong Kong-listed shares struggle to recover from a selloff that’s wiped out about $309 billion in market value since early October.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Warns of Global Oil Glut, Cuts Outlook on Hormuz
item.description: Morgan Stanley cut oil forecasts for the second time in about two weeks as flows through the Strait of Hormuz return faster than expected, while strong US supply and weak Chinese demand raise the risk of a glut.

item.title: Gold Holds Drop as US-Iran Tensions Weigh on Inflation Outlook
item.description: Gold held a decline as traders weighed conflicting signals from the US and Iran ahead of fresh talks to end the war that’s fueled global inflationary pressures.

item.title: Amazon Sued by Australian Consumer Watchdog Over Prime Video Ads
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. has been sued by Australia’s consumer watchdog for introducing advertising to its Prime Video streaming service and then forcing existing subscribers to pay more to avoid the ads.

item.title: Former BOJ Official Sees Next Hike Probably Before December
item.description: The Bank of Japan will probably raise interest rates earlier than most expect as it moves to normalize still excessively accommodative financial conditions, according to the bank’s former Executive Director Kenzo Yamamoto.

item.title: House Passes Online Safety Bill as Senators Seek More Safeguards
item.description: The House on Monday passed legislation to require new online safety protections for children, a sign of growing momentum in Washington to address a widespread concern among US parents even as the measure sets up a clash with senators seeking stronger safeguards.

item.title: China Misses Out on AI Boom as Stocks Trail by Most Since 2001
item.description: After a banner year for Chinese stocks on the back of AI advances, 2026 is not going well.

item.title: Grid Woes Push UK Clean Power Goal Back Five Years, Report Shows
item.description: The UK will probably miss its clean electricity target by five years because of capacity constraints on its grid and could struggle to deliver on promises to cut household energy bills, according to consultant LCP Delta.

item.title: UK Food Inflation Drops to 15-Month Low, Retailers’ Survey Says
item.description: Food inflation has fallen to a 15-month low in the UK, according to a trade association of retailers, the latest sign of easing cost pressures that could prevent the Bank of England from raising interest rates.

item.title: Taiwan Dollar Set to Face Pressure From Record Dividend Payouts
item.description: The Taiwan dollar, which is on track for its first quarterly gain in a year, may face renewed pressure as the US currency strengthens and local companies boost dividend payouts to a record.

item.title: Egypt Edges Closer to $1.6 Billion IMF Payout With Initial Deal
item.description: The International Monetary Fund and Egypt reached a preliminary deal on the country’s latest program review, putting Cairo on track to unlock more than $1.6 billion in financing.

item.title: Raizen Burns More Cash, Sets Steeper Charge Amid Restructure
item.description: Brazilian sugar and fuels company Raizen SA reported a cash burn of 3.4 billion reais ($657 million) for the first three months of 2026 and roughly doubled its impairment charge for the crop year, signaling continued challenges as it proceeds with its debt restructuring plan.

item.title: Comcast’s NBC Spinoff Raises Questions About Businesses’ Future
item.description: Brian Roberts lost out on an opportunity last year to merge NBCUniversal with Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. But the process helped get the chairman and co-chief executive officer of Comcast Corp. thinking about what NBCUniversal would look like on its own.

item.title: Japan Stocks Set to Climb as Yen Hits 40-Year Low: Markets Wrap
item.description: Japanese stocks were set to rise as the yen slid to a four-decade low against the dollar and a rebound in several US technology giants lifted Wall Street.

item.title: Forgent, Backer Offer Stock After Price Doubles Since IPO
item.description: Forgent Power Solutions Inc. and its private equity backer Neos Partners LP are offering to sell about 35 million shares, marking the third stock sale since the power distribution equipment maker went public in February.

item.title: Oil Holds Gain as Iran Seeks Hormuz Control Before Talks With US
item.description: Oil held an advance, as a top Iranian official reiterated the country’s determination to maintain control over the Strait of Hormuz before fresh peace talks with the US.

item.title: Exiled Chinese Tycoon Guo Gets 30 Years in US Prison for Fraud
item.description: Exiled Chinese tycoon Miles Guo, who promoted himself as a crusader against the communist government in Beijing, was ordered to spend 30 years in a US prison for duping investors out of $1 billion.

item.title: Grasshopper Club Zurich Sold by LA Team to Bridge Football
item.description: Bridge Football Group has bought Grasshopper Club Zurich, one of Switzerland’s most storied football teams.

item.title: Supreme Court Affirms Presidential Power, with a Carve-Out for the Fed
item.description: The Supreme Court blocked President Trump’s attempt to remove Fed Governor Lisa Cook for now while expanding his power to fire other officials. What this means for the Fed and others, on the Big Take podcast.

item.title: Takaichi Aims to Shape Legacy With Unprecedented Economic Plan
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is staking her legacy as Japan’s first female premier on an unprecedented plan to reshape the country’s industrial base for decades to come.

item.title: South Korean Financial Firms Fight for Share of AI Pay Bonanza
item.description: South Koreans are getting richer more quickly than anyone else in the world, creating an unprecedented opportunity for the country’s asset managers.

item.title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
item.description: 円が40年ぶり安値、米イラン協議、FRBの独立性、ウォール街沸く、ビットコインETFから資金流出

item.title: Susquehanna Says It Lost Millions to Mystery Insider Traders
item.description: Susquehanna Investment Group is attempting to unmask the identities of individuals it claims made at least $100 million trading on inside information about a Chinese government crackdown on cross-border brokerages last month.

item.title: BofA to Pay SEC $7.5 Million Over Reporting Lapse Allegations
item.description: Bank of America Corp. agreed to pay $7.5 million to the Securities and Exchange Commission to settle allegations that its Merrill Lynch brokerage failed to properly file all required suspicious activity reports, according to a administrative action filed Monday.

item.title: JPMorgan Says Private Muni-Bond Accounts Swell to $1.6 Trillion
item.description: The business of overseeing individually tailored municipal-bond portfolios has continued to grow rapidly, turning those money managers into the biggest holders of state and local government debt, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co.

item.title: Jana Builds Bigger Stake in Fintech Alkami, Pushes for Sale
item.description: Activist investor Jana Partners has built a larger stake in Alkami Technology Inc. and is urging the financial technology company to move forward with a sale, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Six Takeaways From the Supreme Court’s Fed Independence Ruling
item.description: The US Supreme Court on Monday gave President Donald Trump robust legal backing to fire top agency officials, with one glaring exception: the Federal Reserve.

item.title: Argentine Economy Contracted in April as Uneven Growth Persists
item.description: Argentina’s economy contracted in April after a short-lived rebound the previous month, underscoring a trend of uneven growth more than halfway through President Javier Milei’s term.
